title: MemoryWAM: Efficient World Action Modeling with Persistent Memory
summary: World action models (WAMs) jointly model visual foresight and actions conditioned on both current and historical observations, but existing WAMs face a trade-off: efficient methods condition only on a bounded window of recent observations and struggle in non-Markovian environments, while methods that preserve long histories incur time and space costs that grow substantially with sequence length. MemoryWAM is a world action model with efficient persistent memory, using a hybrid memory design that combines recent frames, event-boundary anchor frames, and compact gist tokens that summarize long-range history. A tailored attention mechanism retrieves both detailed short-term and compressed long-term context, supporting memory-dependent decision-making with reduced inference latency and GPU memory usage. Across long-horizon, memory-dependent manipulation tasks in both simulation and the real world, MemoryWAM outperforms strong vision-language-action (VLA) and WAM baselines while maintaining favorable computational efficiency.
